title: [Bug]: 4.2.0 play kube with service-container=true fails to launch all pods defined in a single kube yaml.
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to launch multiple pods defined in a single kube yaml using ```podman-kube@$escaped.service```. However, it only managed to launch the first pod defined in the file. 
```/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman-kube@.service``` shows:
```ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman play kube --replace --service-container=true %I```

If the kube yaml file is launched manually by using the same command, .i.e. 
```podman play kube --replace --service-container=true kube.yaml```, it also shows the same issue. 

If ```--service-container=true``` is not specified, all pods were able to launch. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a kube.yaml containing multiple pods.
2. Use ```systemctl --user start podman-kube@$escaped.service``` to start the pods.

The same issue can also be reproduced by using
```podman play kube --replace --service-container=true kube.yaml```


### Describe the results you received

Only the first pod defined in the kube.yaml was launched. 

### Describe the results you expected

All pods in the kube.yaml should launch.

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out, @cwrx777!  Can you share a reproducer?
Since you're reporting using RHEL.  Note that if you desire a backport, please reach out through the RH customer channels. Without a Bugzilla, we cannot do backports upstream.

I tested it successfully with the latest development version of Podman and the following YAML:
```YAML
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-01-10T14:30:27Z"
  labels:
    app: pod1
  name: pod1
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - top
    image: docker.io/library/alpine:latest
    name: ctr1
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-01-10T14:30:27Z"
  labels:
    app: pod2
  name: pod2
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - top
    image: docker.io/library/alpine:latest
    name: ctr2
```

Both pods start successfully.
I opened https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17058 to show that multi-pod YAMLs work and to make sure we don't regress.
